summary: Betty Cantor’s pristine soundboard captures a scorching April ’71 show highlighted by a rare St. Stephen and a hot China > Rider opener.
review: The recording is universally praised for its crystal‑clear, sweet Betty Cantor soundboard mix, delivering one of the most vivid early‑‘70 tapes. Musically the concert shines with a rare St. Stephen, a blistering China > Rider sequence and an electrifying Hard to Handle, though the set is unusually short and the post‑drums jam fades out.

ai_rating.rationale: Average reviewer rating ~4.25 stars; most comments are glowing about both performance and recording, with only a few notes on tape length and missing jam affecting the score slightly.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Betty Cantor’s soundboard capture is described as sweet, crystal‑clear and high‑end; no distortion or dropout issues are reported.

standout_songs: China > The Midnight Rider > St. Stephen
show_quality.setlist_flow: A rocking first set followed by a second‑set long boogie; the show kicks off the second set with a scorching China > Rider, but overall it runs short (≈1h15) likely due to a venue curfew.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s tone and his improvisational break from Not Fade Away into a jam are highlighted as superb.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil Lesh’s bass on St. Stephen is praised for rumbling “the plant off the mantle.”
band_member_comments.Drums: The post‑drums jam receives high praise, but the tape cuts out just as it builds, leaving listeners wanting more.
